div.h2: HTTP/1.1 Request Wire Format — Annotated Byte Map

g.text: Body region (byte 88+) — empty for GET; req->body_len = 0

div.p: Total request size: 88 bytes (26 request-line + 22 Host-hdr + 25 UA-hdr + 13 Accept-hdr + 2 empty-line) parse_request_line() receives line_len = 24 (bytes 0–23, \r stripped, \n not included) parse_headers() begins scanning at byte 26 (first byte after request-line \n) get_header(req, "host") returns pointer to "localhost:8080" inside req->headers[0].value
method GET = bytes 0–2 (3 B) · SP = byte 3 (0x20) · path /index.html = bytes 4–14 (11 B) · SP = byte 15 · version HTTP/1.1 = bytes 16–23 (8 B) · CRLF = bytes 24–25

text.tspan: No body bytes follow for GET request.
For POST: Content-Length header specifies byte count; body starts at byte 88.

div.p:
= bytes 85–87 (last CRLF of Accept header + this empty CRLF) strstr(buf, "

") matches here → read_request() returns req->body pointer set to byte 88 (first byte after empty line)
